media.ext_alt_text:
"Champs-Élysées: The Iconic Avenue of Elegance and Culture in the Heart of Paris" 
The cat of our story has this question in his mind : 
What does the Champs-Élysées symbolize?

 The name of the Avenue itself speaks of its elegance and luxury. It was named as such during the French Revolution in reference to the Greek underworld.
 According the mythology, the underworld was divided in three and one of its realm was the Champs-Elysées where went the best of men after their deaths. 

The Champs-Élysées in Paris is one of the world's most famous avenues. It stretches 1.17 miles (1.88 km) from the Arc de Triomphe to the Place de la Concorde and is divided by the Rond-Point ("roundabout") des Champs-Élysées.

legacy.name: Pariya

media.ext_alt_text: Stella had heard so much about Earth that she finally decided to visit. She bought what she believed was the perfect Earth outfit—hoodie and jeans—but, to be honest, she couldn't let go of her space helmet. On February 6th, 2025, Stella landed on Earth. According to a travel guide some Earthians (= humans living on Earth) spend their afternoons at a place called a café, having coffee and pastries like chocolate croissants—though she had no idea how to pronounce it. Embracing her adventurous side, she stepped into a café on her first day. Around her, people were working on rectangular- shaped devices, some adorned with a half-bitten apple logo. She found herself enjoying the atmosphere. Then, just a few seats away, an artist named Pariya also known as *Parisketchya*, sat sketching. Pariya immediately recognized Stella from a photo she had seen through Remart. That was the moment she—charmed by Stella's presence —began creating the very sketch you see now.

She found herself enjoying the atmosphere. Then, just a few seats away, an artist named Pariya also known as *Parisketchya*, sat sketching. Pariya immediately recognized Stella from a photo she had seen through Remart. That was the moment she—charmed by Stella's presence —began creating the very sketch you see now.
